The Nut Job (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $64,251,538Details
International Box Office $58,278,428
Worldwide Box Office $122,529,966
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $15,477,825
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $9,047,711
Total $24,525,536
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Surly, a mischievous squirrel, and his rat friend Buddy, plan a nut store heist of outrageous proportions and unwittingly find themselves embroiled in a much more complicated adventure.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$19,405,000 (30.2% of total gross)
Legs:3.31 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:52.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,427 opening theaters/3,472 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $94,057,327

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 17th, 2014 (Wide) by Open Road
International Releases: May 1st, 2014 (Wide) (Estonia)
May 2nd, 2014 (Wide) (Latvia)
May 9th, 2014 (Wide) (Lithuania)
May 15th, 2014 (Wide) (Croatia)
June 5th, 2014 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
Video Release: April 15th, 2014 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for mild action and rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2298, 11/13/2013)
Running Time: 86 minutes
Franchise: The Nut Job
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Director Remaking Their Own Movie, Epilogue, Scene in End Credits, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Food, Heist, Surprise Twist, Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Short Film
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Redrover, Toonbox Entertainment, Gulfstream Pictures, Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism of the Republic of Korea, Korean Small and Medium Business Administration, Korea Venture Investment Corp.
Production Countries: Canada, Republic of Korea
Languages: English
